No appearances.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

RESPONDENT GOOGLE LLC's Motion To Seal - All matters are off calendar in light of automatic stay in bankruptcy, 11 U.S.C. 362(a)(1).

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Petition To Vacate Jams Arbitration Panel'S Corrected Final Award Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 2/6/20 On Cpf-20-516982 Case
Off Calendar Mar-04-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Neil Chatterjee, Esq., Brett Schuman, Esq. and Rachel M. Walsh, Esq. of GOODWIN PROCTER LLP, T:415.733.6021/6128, for Petitioner Levandowski;
Robert A. VAn Nest, Esq., Thomas E. Gorman, Esq., Jo W. Golub, Esq. and Reid Mullen, Esq. of KEKER VAN NEST & PETERS LLP, T:415.391.5400, for Google.

RULING: Prior to the hearing, the Court provided the parties with its tentative ruling on PETITIONER ANTHONY LEVANDOWSKI'S PETITION TO VACATE JAMS ARBITRATION PANEL'S CORRECTED FINAL AWARD. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court decided to re-visit the authorities cited by the parties during the hearing. Therefore,

PETITIONER ANTHONY LEVANDOWSKI'S PETITION TO VACATE JAMS ARBITRATION PANEL'S CORRECTED FINAL AWARD is taken under submission.

Parties are to submit by the end of business on Tuesday, March 3, 2020, a 3-page brief re: statement of decision. Counsel for Google was also instructed to submit a proposed judgment with pre-judgment interest computed up to March 4, 2020 and to include the daily interest rate.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Seal
Off Calendar Feb-18-2020 Continued To Mar-11-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

There being no appearance or objection, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

RESPONDENT GOOGLE LLC'S Motion To Seal is continued to March 11, 2020 per the 2/05/20 stipulation and order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
